edges.description: Loss of neurofibromin removes a major brake on Ras signaling in Schwann-lineage tumor cells.
Persistent Ras signaling contributes to unchecked tumor-cell proliferation.
Loss of CDKN2A/B-mediated checkpoint control permits unchecked cell-cycle entry.
Loss of TP53-mediated genome surveillance facilitates extensive somatic copy-number aberrations.
PRC2 loss removes the repressive H3K27me3 chromatin mark.
Loss of H3K27me3 releases normally repressed developmental transcriptional programs.
Aberrant developmental transcription cooperates with checkpoint loss to sustain malignant proliferation.

evidence.reference_title: PRC2 is recurrently inactivated through EED or SUZ12 loss in malignant peripheral nerve sheath tumors.
evidence.snippet: Additionally, we identified frequent somatic alterations of CDKN2A (81% of all MPNSTs) and NF1 (72% of non-NF1-associated MPNSTs), both of which significantly co-occur with PRC2 alterations.

nodes.description: Deletion of CDKN2A/B is an early cooperative event in malignant progression, weakening cell-cycle restraint before or during transition from atypical neurofibroma to overt MPNST.

evidence.reference_title: Low mutation burden and frequent loss of CDKN2A/B and SMARCA2, but not PRC2, define premalignant neurofibromatosis type 1-associated atypical neurofibromas.
evidence.snippet: The PN-ANF transition is primarily driven by the deletion of CDKN2A/B.

evidence.reference_title: PRC2 is recurrently inactivated through EED or SUZ12 loss in malignant peripheral nerve sheath tumors.
evidence.snippet: Additionally, we identified frequent somatic alterations of CDKN2A (81% of all MPNSTs) and NF1 (72% of non-NF1-associated MPNSTs), both of which significantly co-occur with PRC2 alterations.

nodes.description: Multiple upstream lesions converge on unchecked MPNST cell growth, making proliferative drive a final common consequence of the conventional disease program.

evidence.reference_title: PRC2 is recurrently inactivated through EED or SUZ12 loss in malignant peripheral nerve sheath tumors.
evidence.snippet: Introduction of the lost PRC2 component in a PRC2-deficient MPNST cell line restored H3K27me3 levels and decreased cell growth.

nodes.description: Large-scale somatic copy-number aberrations are a central evolutionary feature of MPNST and help stratify clinically relevant branches of disease.

evidence.reference_title: Genomic Patterns of Malignant Peripheral Nerve Sheath Tumor (MPNST) Evolution Correlate with Clinical Outcome and Are Detectable in Cell-Free DNA.
evidence.snippet: Following biallelic inactivation of NF1, loss of CDKN2A or TP53 with or without inactivation of polycomb repressive complex 2 (PRC2) leads to extensive somatic copy-number aberrations (SCNA).

nodes.description: Loss of PRC2-mediated repression derepresses homeobox and developmental gene programs, reinforcing malignant identity rather than normal Schwann-lineage differentiation.

evidence.reference_title: PRC2 is recurrently inactivated through EED or SUZ12 loss in malignant peripheral nerve sheath tumors.
evidence.snippet: MPNSTs with PRC2 loss showed complete loss of trimethylation at lysine 27 of histone H3 (H3K27me3) and aberrant transcriptional activation of multiple PRC2-repressed homeobox master regulators and their regulated developmental pathways.

evidence.reference_title: PRC2 is recurrently inactivated through EED or SUZ12 loss in malignant peripheral nerve sheath tumors.
evidence.snippet: Using comprehensive genomic approaches, we identified loss-of-function somatic alterations of the Polycomb repressive complex 2 (PRC2) components (EED or SUZ12) in 92% of sporadic, 70% of NF1-associated and 90% of radiotherapy-associated MPNSTs.

nodes.description: PRC2-deficient MPNSTs lose global H3K27 trimethylation, producing a diagnostically useful biomarker state and reshaping downstream gene regulation.

evidence.reference_title: PRC2 is recurrently inactivated through EED or SUZ12 loss in malignant peripheral nerve sheath tumors.
evidence.snippet: MPNSTs with PRC2 loss showed complete loss of trimethylation at lysine 27 of histone H3 (H3K27me3) and aberrant transcriptional activation of multiple PRC2-repressed homeobox master regulators and their regulated developmental pathways.

meta.association: Germline predisposition with biallelic tumor-suppressor loss

evidence.snippet: In 50% of cases, they occur in the context of neurofibromatosis type I, characterized by loss of function mutations to the tumor suppressor neurofibromin; the remainder arise sporadically or following radiation therapy.

nodes.description: Conventional MPNST evolution commonly begins with complete loss of NF1 tumor suppressor function in Schwann-lineage cells, especially in NF1-associated disease, creating the substrate for additional tumor-suppressor and epigenetic lesions.

evidence.reference_title: Genomic Patterns of Malignant Peripheral Nerve Sheath Tumor (MPNST) Evolution Correlate with Clinical Outcome and Are Detectable in Cell-Free DNA.
evidence.snippet: Following biallelic inactivation of NF1, loss of CDKN2A or TP53 with or without inactivation of polycomb repressive complex 2 (PRC2) leads to extensive somatic copy-number aberrations (SCNA).

nodes.description: Conventional MPNSTs recurrently inactivate the PRC2 core components SUZ12 or EED, creating a major epigenetic branch of disease evolution across NF1, sporadic, and radiation-associated settings.

evidence.reference_title: PRC2 is recurrently inactivated through EED or SUZ12 loss in malignant peripheral nerve sheath tumors.
evidence.snippet: Using comprehensive genomic approaches, we identified loss-of-function somatic alterations of the Polycomb repressive complex 2 (PRC2) components (EED or SUZ12) in 92% of sporadic, 70% of NF1-associated and 90% of radiotherapy-associated MPNSTs.

evidence.reference_title: Genetics of human malignant peripheral nerve sheath tumors.
evidence.snippet: Recent advances in genomic studies identified previously unrecognized critical involvement of polycomb repressor complex 2 (PRC2) core components SUZ12 and EED in transition to malignancy.

nodes.description: NF1 loss and additional secondary lesions converge on sustained Ras-pathway signaling, which reinforces malignant growth and cooperates with downstream cell-cycle and chromatin dysregulation.

evidence.reference_title: The genomic landscape of malignant peripheral nerve sheath tumors: diverse drivers of Ras pathway activation.
evidence.snippet: Notably, we also identified frequent Ras pathway activating somatic mutations outside of these previously reported recurrently mutated genes.

meta.association: Somatic loss of function in epithelioid subtype

evidence.reference_title: Recurrent SMARCB1 Inactivation in Epithelioid Malignant Peripheral Nerve Sheath Tumors.
evidence.snippet: Epithelioid malignant peripheral nerve sheath tumors (EMPNST) are characterized by diffuse S-100 and SOX10 positivity, frequent immunohistochemical loss of SMARCB1 expression (70%), and rare association with neurofibromatosis type 1.

nodes.description: Epithelioid MPNST follows a subtype-specific chromatin-disruption branch in which SMARCB1 loss is recurrent, in contrast to the conventional PRC2-loss signature of most non-epithelioid tumors.

evidence.reference_title: Recurrent SMARCB1 Inactivation in Epithelioid Malignant Peripheral Nerve Sheath Tumors.
evidence.snippet: Sequencing identified SMARCB1 gene inactivation in 12/16 (75%) EMPNST and all 5 (100%) ESCW through homozygous deletion (N=8), nonsense (N=7), frameshift (N=2), or splice site (N=2) mutations

evidence.reference_title: PRC2 is recurrently inactivated through EED or SUZ12 loss in malignant peripheral nerve sheath tumors.
evidence.snippet: Using comprehensive genomic approaches, we identified loss-of-function somatic alterations of the Polycomb repressive complex 2 (PRC2) components (EED or SUZ12) in 92% of sporadic, 70% of NF1-associated and 90% of radiotherapy-associated MPNSTs.

evidence.reference_title: The genomic landscape of malignant peripheral nerve sheath tumors: diverse drivers of Ras pathway activation.
evidence.snippet: We report recurrent mutations in NF1, SUZ12, EED, TP53 and CDKN2A in our study cohort.

nodes.description: TP53 disruption removes an additional tumor-suppressive barrier, promoting malignant progression and large-scale genomic instability.

evidence.reference_title: The genomic landscape of malignant peripheral nerve sheath tumors: diverse drivers of Ras pathway activation.
evidence.snippet: We report recurrent mutations in NF1, SUZ12, EED, TP53 and CDKN2A in our study cohort.
